title: The Girl — Origins and Role
text: The Girl is a nameless, mute child imprisoned within the dungeons. She is the daughter of the New God Nilvan and Le'garde, conceived in a dream when Nilvan visited Le'garde hoping to create a being that would transcend humanity. The Girl bears an Ancient One soul, a dormant spark of divine potential. She appears deeply traumatized, unused to kindness. She is found in a cage early in the dungeons and can be recruited as a party member. She is very weak in combat but will sacrifice herself to protect party members below 25% HP. She can be given a Peculiar Doll and a Dagger, after which she speaks her only line: 'y-yes.' She can be sold to Pocketcat or sacrificed at ritual circles.

title: The Girl — Fate and Ascension
text: If the Girl is brought to the Altar of Darkness within the body of the God of the Depths, she begins the process of ascension to divinity, shedding her human skin and becoming the God of Fear and Hunger. This requires destroying the three hearts of the God of the Depths and descending to the Heart of Darkness. Her ascension is canonical — she appears in Fear & Hunger 2: Termina as a revered deity. This is Ending A. Nilvan hoped her daughter would become a beacon of hope for mankind, transcending her own limited lifespan as a New God.

title: Le'garde — The Prophesied One
text: Le'garde is the captain of the Knights of the Midnight Sun and the central figure driving the four protagonists into the dungeons. He is a charismatic leader who believes in unifying the kingdom through non-violent means, but paradoxically was willing to invade Oldegård and kill to obtain the Cube of the Depths. He was captured by Rondon and imprisoned in the dungeons where Trortur tortured him. His amnesia after being rescued is a facade — he manipulates his savior into helping him reach ascension. Each aspect of his suffering was orchestrated: torment through torture, enlightenment through the Cube, endlessness through Nilvan, and domination through his military campaign.

title: Le'garde — Fate and Endings
text: Le'garde's survival determines several endings. If found alive and kept in the party, he can ascend via the Throne of Ascension. In Ending C (The Yellow King), the protagonist rejects his godhood and kills him — party escapes, scarred forever. In Ending C-II (The Prophecy), the protagonist kneels — Le'garde unifies the western world and brings prosperity as the Yellow King. In Ending E, finding Le'garde dead or alive allows escape from the dungeons. Le'garde's future self exists in Termina as Kaiser. If Ragnvaldr is in the party, a confrontation occurs between them.

title: Buckman — The Crown Prince
text: Lord Ludwig Buckman is the Crown Prince of the Kingdom of Rondon. He led a party consisting of Ser Seymor, Jeanne, and Ser Seril into the Dungeons of Fear and Hunger to investigate what happened to Captain Rudimer. The party was ambushed and dispersed soon after entering. Buckman is found alone on Level 1 Inner Hall, having escaped from the torturer Trortur. He is panicked and advises the player that stealth is the only way to survive. He has lost his sense of time and does not know if he has been trapped for weeks, months, or years.

title: Buckman — Questline
text: Buckman's quest involves reuniting him with his three companions. Killing Trortur and informing Buckman triggers his move to Level 2 basement to reunite with Ser Seymor. This must be done before visiting Ma'habre or Buckman is killed by Trortur — his body found cut in half on a torture table. Jeanne is found in Ma'habre being attacked by a Lord of Flies — rescuing her returns her to the basement. Ser Seril is found as an enemy in past Ma'habre — using a Potion of Mind on him in combat restores his sanity and he reunites. After all four are reunited, they leave the dungeons, leaving Buckman's letter and a Ring of Wraiths as a gift. Buckman asks the player to end Captain Rudimer's suffering if found.

title: Cahara — The Mercenary
text: Cahara is a playable character from Jettaiah in the Eastern Sanctuaries. Born with the soul of the endless, he was abandoned at birth and grew up choosing between crime and honest trade. He joined mercenary bands and eventually a notorious highwayman's gang, raiding across the lands. In Rondon, he fell in love with Celeste, a prostitute in indentured servitude, and seeks money to buy her freedom. Rondon officials hired him to extract Le'garde from the dungeons. Cahara is known for dirty tactics, craftiness, and doing whatever brings silver. He has no phobias.

title: D'arce — The Knight
text: D'arce Cataliss is a playable character from the Kingdom of Rondon. Born with the soul of domination, she was raised in nobility and became a holy knight of Alll-mer. Disillusioned by Rondon's pursuit of power benefiting only the elite, she left the army and joined the Knights of the Midnight Sun. She fell deeply in love with Le'garde, though he viewed her only as a valuable subordinate. She enters the dungeons to rescue him. D'arce is a formidable warrior excelling in close combat with swords and heavy armor. She has no phobias.

title: Enki — The Dark Priest
text: Enki Ankarian is a playable character, a dark priest from a lineage with centuries-long occult connections. Born with the soul of the enlightened, he pursued forbidden knowledge endlessly. During his initiation, he was forced to fight his twin sister with ceremonial knives — she spared him. Playing as Enki, the player can choose to kill her anyway to appease the high priests, or accept defeat and be cast into a well where he learns the language of insects. He enters the dungeons seeking ancient knowledge and the secrets of Ma'habre. He bears no burden of morality or ethics and has edge in blood magic, but his devotion to magic left him physically weak.

title: Ragnvaldr — The Outlander
text: Ragnvaldr is a playable character from the northern Kingdom of Oldegård. Born with the soul of the tormented, he was destined to face relentless struggle, which forged his strength and iron will. He sailed to the dark continent of Vinland and survived, bringing back the Cube of the Depths. The Knights of the Midnight Sun invaded Oldegård to steal the Cube, massacring his tribe and killing his wife Hilde and son Björn. He enters the dungeons seeking vengeance on Le'garde and to recover the Cube. Ragnvaldr is an expert hunter and survivalist with proficiency in bows and axes. He has no phobias.

title: Trortur — The Torturer
text: Trortur is the insane torturer of the dungeons. He tortured Le'garde and is responsible for tormenting prisoners in the dungeon's lower levels. Buckman escaped from him. The player can encounter Trortur in the prisons. Killing him is part of Buckman's quest — if he is not killed before the player visits Ma'habre, Buckman is found dead on a torture table, cut in half. The player can also betray Buckman to Trortur in exchange for a Level 2F key and two Light Blue Vials.

title: Nosramus — The Ancient Alchemist
text: Nosramus is an ancient alchemist residing deep in the dungeons. He is knowledgeable about the dungeon's secrets, alchemical recipes, and the history of Ma'habre. He describes Le'garde's plan to ascend as 'not necessarily the right path, but an interesting event to unfold.' Nosramus provides crucial lore about the New Gods' souls and the Throne of Ascension. He is one of the few genuinely helpful NPCs in the dungeons.

title: Pocketcat — The Sinister Merchant
text: Pocketcat is a mysterious merchant NPC who appears in the dungeons and Ma'habre. He offers unique items and can take the Girl from the party if the player agrees to sell her. If the player refuses twice, Pocketcat appears at the Golden Temple after the Nameless Figurine is defeated — refusing a third time causes him to give up and disappear forever. Accepting results in the Girl being permanently removed. Pocketcat speaks in riddles and his true nature is ambiguous.
